# Card Templates

Card templates determine which fields display on card fronts and backs, and control card generation when fields contain text. Template adjustments allow you to modify the design and styling of multiple cards simultaneously.

## The Templates Screen

Access card templates by clicking the **Cards…** button in the editing screen. Switch between Front template, Back template, and Styling using Ctrl+1, Ctrl+2, and Ctrl+3 respectively.

Templates use HTML (web page markup language), while the styling section employs CSS (web page styling language). A preview panel on the right shows the current card's front and back. The preview content depends on your context:

- When adding notes, it reflects typed text from the Add Notes window
- When editing notes, it shows the note's current content
- When accessing from Tools → Manage Note Types, field names appear in parentheses instead of content

## Options

An Options button at the top right provides several features:

- **Deck Override**: Reassign cards generated from a card type to a different deck than specified in the Add Notes window. This proves useful for separating card types into different decks, such as "placing production cards in one deck and recognition cards in another" when studying languages.

- **Browser Appearance**: Configure simplified templates for the Question and Answer columns in the browser interface.
